content: Indications: T2DM (all agents); obesity (liraglutide 3mg, semaglutide 2.4mg); ASCVD risk reduction (liraglutide, semaglutide, dulaglutide proven). Dosing requires gradual titration to minimize GI side effects: liraglutide start 0.6mg daily, increase weekly to 1.2-1.8mg (diabetes) or 3mg (obesity); semaglutide SC start 0.25mg weekly, increase monthly to 1-2mg (diabetes) or 2.4mg (obesity); dulaglutide start 0.75mg weekly, titrate to 1.5-4.5mg. Common adverse effects: nausea (most common), vomiting, diarrhea, constipation - usually transient. Contraindications: personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 (rodent C-cell tumor risk); caution with pancreatitis history. Monitor for gallbladder disease. Renal function monitoring advised. Avoid combining with DPP-4 inhibitors (redundant mechanism). Can combine with basal insulin; may allow insulin dose reduction.
